Class Game

java.lang.Object
javafx.application.Application
Game
public class Game extends javafx.application.Application
This class builds the piccross UI and contains the controller for the UI to interact with the game logic.
Author:
Ryanh

    • setTime

      public void setTime​(int time)
      Sets the time field with the elapsed time.
      Parameters:
      time - The elapsed time since the timer started.

    • setScore

      public void setScore()
      Sets the current score on the score board.

    • updateGameGrid

      public void updateGameGrid​(int index, boolean answer)
      The method updates the game grid when the user clicks on a button. In non mark mode, If the user selects the right square it will turn black and the score will be updated. If incorrent it will have a red x on it. If it in mark mode, the square will turn white if the selection is valid and will have a red x if the selection in not valid. The method also checks to see if the user has ended the game on each click. It will put up an alert if the user has reach end of game conditions.
      Parameters:
      index - This is the index of the button being pressed.
      answer - This is whether the button is a valid choice or not depending on the mode.

    • setHints

      public void setHints()
      Sets both the vertical and horizontal hints on the UI.

    • solution

      public void solution()
      Provides the solution to the user by writing valid or empty square on each button.

    • setGrid

      public void setGrid()
      Writes button grid to UI.

      public void resetHints()
      Resets the hints on the board when a new game is made.
